name: ceiling_guard_lead_time_check
description: Warm day, ODE enabled, HVAC off. Two temp_update events test the ceiling guard lead-time gate (Gap 1). At 09:00: indoor=73F, outdoor=90F — ODE projects no breach in 4-hour window (solar gain too weak at hour=9) → guard does NOT fire. At 12:00: indoor=74.6F, outdoor=90F — breach predicted at step 0 (30 min), within lead time → ceiling guard fires.
unsupported_reason: Superseded by #249 (band model). The #249 band model holds the comfort_cool ceiling natively — the band's upper bound triggers AC escalation when indoor approaches or exceeds comfort_cool, making the ODE lead-time ceiling guard a passive backstop rather than the primary ceiling defender. The ceiling_guard_fired event type that this scenario asserts was designed for the old standalone simulator (tools/simulate.py Gap 1 implementation). Since #236 replaced the simulator with the real production AutomationEngine, the engine no longer emits ceiling_guard_fired as a proactive ODE lead-time decision when indoor first crosses comfort_cool — it returns no_decision because the band's own ceiling logic handles it. The p3_* golden scenarios (p3_warm_day_dual_thermostat_comfort_band, warm_day_ceiling_breach_ac_defense) cover ceiling defense under the band model. This scenario documents a deliberate scope decision: the standalone ODE lead-time gate as a scenario-level assertion is superseded; do not re-implement as a Tier-A scenario.
notes: Tests Gap 1: ceiling guard lead-time check in simulate.py.
Gap 1 is already implemented: if breach_hours > lead_min/60, guard does not fire.

09:00 assertion: ODE at hour=9 with indoor=73F gives dT=-0.075°F/step (solar < passive loss) — indoor DECREASES, never reaches breach threshold (74.5F). breach_idx=None → within_lead_time=False → no decision recorded → no_decision.

12:00 assertion: ODE at hour=12 with indoor=74.6F. Step 0: dT=(-0.04)*(90-74.6)+0.946=0.33; T=74.765>74.5 → breach_idx=0, breach_hours=0.0. indoor > comfort_cool → lead_min=((74.6-74.0)/11.8)*60*1.3=3.97, clamped to 30.0. within_lead_time=(0.0<=0.5)=True → ceiling_guard_fired.
Both assertions are logic-track (simulator_support: true). Gap 1 is closed in simulate.py.
k_active_cool=-11.8 (negative = cooling contribution to dT). lead-time uses abs(k_active_cool)=11.8.

events.note: Morning: indoor=73F, outdoor=90F, HVAC off. ODE at hour=9: solar gain < passive loss → indoor decreases. No breach predicted in 4-hour window.
Noon: indoor=74.6F (above comfort_cool=74F), outdoor=90F, HVAC off. ODE at hour=12: breach at step 0 (30min). Lead_min=30min. Guard fires.

assertions.reason: ODE projects no breach in 4-hour window at 09:00 (indoor=73F decreasing). Ceiling guard does not fire. No prior events — _outcome_at returns no_decision.

ODE at hour=12: indoor=74.6F → step-0 breach (74.765F > 74.5F threshold). breach_hours=0.0 <= lead_min/60=0.5. Ceiling guard fires: switches to cool mode at comfort_cool=74F. Superseded by #249 band model; see p3_* goldens.

verdict.summary: Ceiling guard lead-time gate: guard suppressed when breach is far (no breach in 4hr window); guard fires when breach is imminent (30 min)
verdict.observed_behavior: no_decision at 09:00 (no breach) → ceiling_guard_fired at 12:00 (breach at 30min, within lead time)
verdict.expected_behavior: Ceiling guard only activates when ODE predicts an imminent comfort ceiling breach, not proactively hours in advance
